generatedSentence: A wealthy thinker, known for his impressive aplomb, strictly viewed the intricate glowing mechanisms on his nightstand and tabletop, planning to invest in their restoration and housing, despite an ongoing backlog visible on his dashboard, made of curiously shaped driftwood. He spent many an hourly bedtime, anyway, observing how the large elephants might enjoy their bubble anywhere, even contemplating their future, before finally unpacking. This thoughtful approach was an illustrative display of his character. However, a rebellious phantom rhino, accompanied by a snarling rattlesnake and a powerful gemsbok, presented significant resistances. This horrid misdeed led to an outcry, causing difficulty for the restoration project and threatening to injure the anemic workers. The hunter, using a mattock, tried to negate the problem, but the matter was complicated by gnomes who had chewed through vital power lines, racking up a fierce punishment. The company had to oversee the lechery that violated all the laws. The climax arrived when javelins began knocking against their defenses, thrown by feckless jokers from the tramway in the moonlight. A massive whirlpool formed, its acid churning as a naked caterpillar, acting like a monkey, vibrated a critical support beam. The widowmaker, a legendary figure, appeared from nowhere in the night, his glowing jacinth eyes fixated on the scene. He had to unhook the main power conduit and jettison the unstable section, bringing a lively justice to the chaos. After the dramatic resolution, the surface of the land was finally at peace, though emotional embanks of borsch and kimchi were necessary victuals for the weary team. Kisses of bright rainbows stretched across the sky, a fairy's touch after the heating turmoil, as a bullfrog watched from washed-up logs. The lumberjacks and even the heckler, alongside a young nephew with a healing nosebleed, all interacted, understanding that flexible endings were often the most satisfying. The trolleybus now ran smoothly, its horn a triumphant sound, through the schoolroom district.
panelSentences: A wealthy thinker, known for his impressive aplomb, strictly viewed the intricate glowing mechanisms on his nightstand and tabletop, planning to invest in their restoration and housing, despite an ongoing backlog visible on his dashboard, made of curiously shaped driftwood. He spent many an hourly bedtime, anyway, observing how the large elephants might enjoy their bubble anywhere, even contemplating their future, before finally unpacking. This thoughtful approach was an illustrative display of his character.

However, a rebellious phantom rhino, accompanied by a snarling rattlesnake and a powerful gemsbok, presented significant resistances. This horrid misdeed led to an outcry, causing difficulty for the restoration project and threatening to injure the anemic workers. The hunter, using a mattock, tried to negate the problem, but the matter was complicated by gnomes who had chewed through vital power lines, racking up a fierce punishment. The company had to oversee the lechery that violated all the laws.

The climax arrived when javelins began knocking against their defenses, thrown by feckless jokers from the tramway in the moonlight. A massive whirlpool formed, its acid churning as a naked caterpillar, acting like a monkey, vibrated a critical support beam. The widowmaker, a legendary figure, appeared from nowhere in the night, his glowing jacinth eyes fixated on the scene. He had to unhook the main power conduit and jettison the unstable section, bringing a lively justice to the chaos.

After the dramatic resolution, the surface of the land was finally at peace, though emotional embanks of borsch and kimchi were necessary victuals for the weary team. Kisses of bright rainbows stretched across the sky, a fairy's touch after the heating turmoil, as a bullfrog watched from washed-up logs. The lumberjacks and even the heckler, alongside a young nephew with a healing nosebleed, all interacted, understanding that flexible endings were often the most satisfying. The trolleybus now ran smoothly, its horn a triumphant sound, through the schoolroom district.
rawPanelSentences: A wealthy thinker, known for his impressive aplomb, strictly viewed the intricate glowing mechanisms on his nightstand and tabletop, planning to invest in their restoration and housing, despite an ongoing backlog visible on his dashboard, made of curiously shaped driftwood. He spent many an hourly bedtime, anyway, observing how the large elephants might enjoy their bubble anywhere, even contemplating their future, before finally unpacking. This thoughtful approach was an illustrative display of his character.

However, a rebellious phantom rhino, accompanied by a snarling rattlesnake and a powerful gemsbok, presented significant resistances. This horrid misdeed led to an outcry, causing difficulty for the restoration project and threatening to injure the anemic workers. The hunter, using a mattock, tried to negate the problem, but the matter was complicated by gnomes who had chewed through vital power lines, racking up a fierce punishment. The company had to oversee the lechery that violated all the laws.

The climax arrived when javelins began knocking against their defenses, thrown by feckless jokers from the tramway in the moonlight. A massive whirlpool formed, its acid churning as a naked caterpillar, acting like a monkey, vibrated a critical support beam. The widowmaker, a legendary figure, appeared from nowhere in the night, his glowing jacinth eyes fixated on the scene. He had to unhook the main power conduit and jettison the unstable section, bringing a lively justice to the chaos.

After the dramatic resolution, the surface of the land was finally at peace, though emotional embanks of borsch and kimchi were necessary victuals for the weary team. Kisses of bright rainbows stretched across the sky, a fairy's touch after the heating turmoil, as a bullfrog watched from washed-up logs. The lumberjacks and even the heckler, alongside a young nephew with a healing nosebleed, all interacted, understanding that flexible endings were often the most satisfying. The trolleybus now ran smoothly, its horn a triumphant sound, through the schoolroom district.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A cyber-samurai with glowing jacinth eyes, wearing an intricate, acid-resistant armored kimono and carrying a data-flute. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone cyber-samurai, deep in thought, gazing at a holographic map projected from a nightstand, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The cyber-samurai confronts a towering, rebellious phantom rhino, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The cyber-samurai deftly deflects javelins hurled by shadowy figures near a swirling whirlpool, intense mid-shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cyber-samurai stands triumphant amidst blooming neon rainbows, a serene smile hidden behind their mask, long shot.

definitions.definition: To look at.

A recorder, also a woodwind instrument.
A glass with a long, narrow bowl and a long stem, used for drinking wine, especially champagne.

Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: Food fit for human consumption.
(in the plural) Food supplies; provisions.
Grain of any kind.
To provide with food; to provision.
To lay in food supplies.
To eat.
To clean with water.
definitions.example: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

definitions.definition: To move or erode by the force of water in motion.
definitions.example: Heavy rains wash a road or an embankment.

definitions.definition: To separate valuable material (such as gold) from worthless material by the action of flowing water.
To clean oneself with water.
definitions.example: I wash every morning after getting up.

definitions.definition: To cover with water or any liquid; to wet; to fall on and moisten.
definitions.example: Waves wash the shore.

definitions.definition: To move with a lapping or swashing sound; to lap or splash.
definitions.example: to hear the water washing

definitions.definition: To be eroded or carried away by the action of water.
To be cogent, convincing; to withstand critique.
To bear without injury the operation of being washed.
definitions.example: Some calicoes do not wash.

definitions.definition: To be wasted or worn away by the action of water, as by a running or overflowing stream, or by the dashing of the sea; said of road, a beach, etc.
To cover with a thin or watery coat of colour; to tint lightly and thinly.
To overlay with a thin coat of metal.
definitions.example: steel washed with silver

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
Like a fairy; fanciful, whimsical, delicate.
A termination or conclusion.
The last part of something.
(grammar) The last morpheme of a word, added to some base to make an inflected form (such as -ing in "ending").
The overside or up-side of a flat object such as a table, or of a liquid.
The outside hull of a tangible object.
Outward or external appearance.
definitions.example: On the surface, the spy looked like a typical businessman.

definitions.definition: The locus of an equation (especially one with exactly two degrees of freedom) in a more-than-two-dimensional space.
(fortification) That part of the side which is terminated by the flank prolonged, and the angle of the nearest bastion.
To provide something with a surface.
To apply a surface to something.
To rise to the surface.
To bring to the surface.
To come out of hiding.
For information or facts to become known.
To make information or facts known.
To work a mine near the surface.
To appear or be found.
An individual who has anemia.
Of, pertaining to, or suffering from anemia.
(by extension) Weak; listless; lacking power, vigor, vitality, or colorfulness.
A multicoloured arch in the sky, produced by prismatic refraction of light within droplets of rain in the air.
Any prismatic refraction of light showing a spectrum of colours.
(often used with “of”) A wide assortment; a varied multitude.
definitions.example: a rainbow of possibilities

definitions.definition: An illusion, mirage.
definitions.example: Many electoral promises are rainbows, vanishing soon after poll day.

definitions.definition: A curveball, particularly a slow one.
In Texas hold 'em or Omaha hold 'em, a flop that contains three different suits.
Rainbow trout.
To brighten with, or as with, a rainbow; to pattern with the colours of the rainbow.
To take the appearance of a rainbow.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
A touch with the lips, usually to express love or affection, or as a greeting.
An 'X' mark placed at the end of a letter or other type of message.
A type of filled chocolate candy, shaped as if someone had kissed the top. See Hershey's Kisses.
Making, or tending to make, a positive impression; having power to impress
definitions.example: an impressive movie

definitions.definition: Capable of being impressed.
Appealing.
Something produced each hour.
definitions.example: She never reads her hourly report, but let her hourlies be fifteen minutes late and she raises a ruckus.

definitions.definition: Occurring every hour.
definitions.example: There are hourly express buses.

definitions.definition: Unsalaried, paid by the hour; (by extension) blue-collar.
At intervals of an hour.
definitions.example: Express buses depart hourly.

definitions.definition: A beetroot soup that can be served hot or cold, usually with sour cream.
Any similar sour soup made of other ingredients such as sorrel, cabbage, hogweed
In a bright manner.
definitions.example: The sun was shining brightly, making me squint.

definitions.definition: Any of various frogs having a croak that resembles the bellow of a cow or bull.
Bare, not covered by clothing.
definitions.example: She was as naked as the day she was born.

definitions.definition: Lacking some clothing; clothed only in underwear.
Glib, without decoration, put bluntly.
definitions.example: The naked facts lay there on the table, enclosed within the files.

definitions.definition: Characterized by the nakedness of the people concerned or to whom the described noun is attributed.
Unarmed.
Unaided, unaccompanied.
Unprotected, uncovered; (by extension) without a condom.
definitions.example: I entered her naked and came in her too.

definitions.definition: (of a derivative contract) Where the writer (seller) does not own the underlying asset to cover the contract.
definitions.example: a naked put, a naked call

definitions.definition: Resourceless, poor, lacking means.
(with “of”) Lacking or devoid of something.
Blank, clean, empty.
(of land, rocks, or plants) Barren, having no foliage, unvegetated.
Uncomfortable or vulnerable, as if missing something important.
definitions.example: I feel naked without my mobile phone.

definitions.definition: (of food or other consumer products) Without any additives, or without some component that would usually be included.
definitions.example: Naked Bacon (a brand without nitrates or phosphates)

definitions.definition: Of a singularity, not hidden within an event horizon and thus observable from other parts of spacetime.
An arrangement, categorization or classification of things.
A disposing of or getting rid of something.
The power to use something or someone.
The larva of a butterfly or moth; leafworm.
definitions.example: The bird just ate that green caterpillar.

definitions.definition: A vehicle with a caterpillar track; a crawler.

Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.
To resonate.
definitions.example: Her mind was vibrating with excitement.

definitions.definition: To brandish; to swing to and fro.
definitions.example: to vibrate a sword or a staff

definitions.definition: To mark or measure by moving to and fro.
definitions.example: a pendulum vibrating seconds

definitions.definition: To affect with vibratory motion; to set in vibration.
To please or impress someone.
To use vibrato.
To speak boastfully.
To speak boastfully about.
To boast of; to make a vain display of; to display with ostentation.
Highly or widely praised or boasted about.
Something which or someone who takes the lives of men; a lethal hazard that affects mostly men or is specific to a primarily male trade.
The detached or broken limb of a tree, a hazard to those walking underneath.
(The left coronary artery, or its anterior interventricular branch, occlusion of which is likely to cause a fatal heart attack)
Something that is flexible.
Capable of being flexed or bent without breaking; able to be turned or twisted without breaking.
Willing or prone to give way to the influence of others; not invincibly rigid or obstinate.
Capable or being adapted or molded in some way.
definitions.example: This was a principle more flexible to their purpose. -Rogers.

definitions.definition: A mammal of the order Proboscidea, having a trunk, and two large ivory tusks jutting from the upper jaw.
Anything huge and ponderous.
Used when counting to add length, so that each count takes about one second
definitions.example: Let's play hide and seek. I'll count. One elephant, two elephant, three elephant...

definitions.definition: Ivory.
A large size of paper, measuring 23 by 28 inches, or sometimes 22 by 30, or (double elephant paper) 26 by 40 inches.
To receive pleasure or satisfaction from something
definitions.example: Enjoy your holidays!   I enjoy dancing.

definitions.definition: To have the use or benefit of something.
definitions.example: I plan to go travelling while I still enjoy good health.

definitions.definition: To be satisfied or receive pleasure.
definitions.example: I enjoyed a lot.

definitions.definition: To have sexual intercourse with.
In or at any location or an unknown location.
definitions.example: I don't know where I left my keys. They could be anywhere.

definitions.definition: To (in the direction of) any location or an unknown location.
definitions.example: Anywhere you go in France, there will be bread and wine with dinner.

definitions.definition: Any location or an unknown location.
definitions.example: Anywhere is better than here!

definitions.definition: A tomato-vinegar-based sauce, sometimes containing spices, onion or garlic, and (especially in the US) sweeteners.
Such a sauce more generally (not necessarily based on tomatoes).
definitions.example: fish ketchup; fruit ketchup; mushroom ketchup

definitions.definition: Admired, distinguished, respected, or well-known, especially due to past achievements or noble qualities.
A person whose work is to fell trees.
A lumberjacket.
A spasm of the diaphragm, or the resulting sound.
definitions.example: There was a loud hiccup from the back of the room and the class erupted in laughter.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any spasm or sudden change.
A minor setback.
definitions.example: There's been a slight hiccup in the processing of this quarter's results.

definitions.definition: To produce a hiccup; have the hiccups.
To say with a hiccup.
definitions.example: "I haven't touched a drop, officer," the suspect hiccupped.

definitions.definition: To produce an abortive sound like a hiccup.
definitions.example: The car engine hiccupped but wouldn't start.

definitions.definition: Any substance produced by one tissue and conveyed by the bloodstream to another to effect physiological activity.
A synthetic compound with the same activity.
Any similar substance in plants.
To treat with hormones.
To keep within a structure or container.
definitions.example: The car is housed in the garage.

definitions.definition: To admit to residence; to harbor/harbour.
To take shelter or lodging; to abide; to lodge.
To dwell within one of the twelve astrological houses.
To contain or cover mechanical parts.
To drive to a shelter.
To deposit and cover, as in the grave.
To stow in a safe place; to take down and make safe.
definitions.example: to house the upper spars

definitions.definition: To eat.
A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.
A small spherical cavity in a solid material.
definitions.example: bubbles in window glass, or in a lens

definitions.definition: (by extension) Anything resembling a hollow sphere.
Anything lacking firmness or solidity; a cheat or fraud; an empty project.
A period of intense speculation in a market, causing prices to rise quickly to irrational levels as the metaphorical bubble expands, and then fall even more quickly as the bubble bursts.
definitions.example: dot-com bubble

definitions.definition: The emotional and/or physical atmosphere in which the subject is immersed.
An officer's station in a prison dormitory, affording views on all sides.
Someone who has been ‘bubbled’ or fooled; a dupe.
A small, hollow, floating bead or globe, formerly used for testing the strength of spirits.
The globule of air in the chamber of a spirit level.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a bubble?!

definitions.definition: A Greek.
Any of the small magnetized areas that make up bubble memory.

The point in a poker tournament when the last player without a prize loses all their chips and leaves the game, leaving only players that are going to win prizes. (e.g., if the last remaining 9 players win prizes, then the point when the 10th player leaves the tournament)
definitions.example: Many players tend to play timidly (not play many hands) around the bubble, to keep their chips and last longer in the game.

definitions.definition: A group of people who are in quarantine together.
To produce bubbles, to rise up in bubbles (such as in foods cooking or liquids boiling).
To churn or foment, as if wishing to rise to the surface.
definitions.example: Rage bubbled inside him.

definitions.definition: To rise through a medium or system, similar to the way that bubbles rise in liquid.
To cheat, delude.
To cry, weep.
To pat a baby on the back so as to cause it to belch.
To cause to feel as if bubbling or churning.
To express in a bubbly or lively manner.
To form into a protruding round shape.
To cover with bubbles.
To bubble in; to mark a response on a form by filling in a circular area (‘bubble’).
(collective) Items that have been or are about to be ejected from a boat or balloon.
The action of jettisoning items.
To eject from a boat, submarine, aircraft, spaceship or hot-air balloon, so as to lighten the load.
definitions.example: The ballooners had to jettison all of their sand bags to make it over the final hill.

definitions.definition: To let go or get rid of as being useless or defective.
The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
definitions.example: the justice of a description

definitions.definition: The ideal of fairness, impartiality, etc., especially with regard to the punishment of wrongdoing.
definitions.example: Justice was served.

definitions.definition: Judgment and punishment of a party who has allegedly wronged another.
definitions.example: to demand justice

definitions.definition: The civil power dealing with law.
definitions.example: Ministry of Justice

definitions.definition: A title given to judges of certain courts; capitalized as a title.
definitions.example: Mr. Justice Krever presides over the appellate court

definitions.definition: Correctness, conforming to reality or rules.
A haemorrhage from the nose; most specifically, blood flow exiting the nostrils that originates from the nasal cavity.
A nerd or a geek or a dork
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
A bus, powered via overhead electric cables, that does not run on tracks
That which was done that should not have been, ranging from any sin or moral offense to various degrees of crime.
definitions.example: The petty misdeeds of his youth came back to haunt him when he ran for political office and his character was smeared.
